LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LISA LANDEY MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. THE DEFENDANT WAS ADDED BY AMENDMENT TO COMPLAINT ON MAY 8, 2009 AND SERVED LESS THAN ONE MONTH LATER. THE ACTION IS DEEMED COMMENCED AS TO THE NEW DEFENDANT FROM THE DATE OF THE FILING OF THE AMENDMENT. (SEE HENNESSEY'S TAVERN, INC. VS. AMERICAN AIR FILTER CO. (1988) 204 CAL.APP. 3D 1351, 1357, WHERE NEW DEFENDANT WAS ALLEGEDLY ALTER EGO OF DEFENDANT NAMED IN ORIGINAL COMPLAINT.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
CONT. ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-17-2010 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion Andmotion For Order Dismissing Pltf'S Complaint For Failure To Obey This Court'S Prior Order And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. Tentative adopted. DEFENDANT LISA LANDEY Notice Of Motion Andmotion For Order Dismissing Plaintiff's Complaint For Failure To Obey This Court's Prior Order And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions is not opposed, and motion is granted. Sanctions in the sum of $290 are awarded against plaintiff, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge: Curtis Karnow. Not Reported.

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2010 Continued To May-20-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Form Interrogatories And Responses And Production Of Documents And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Thomas J. Feeney, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Lisa Landey
Joseph R. Olin, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Cal Sorenson

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Defendant Lisa Landey's motion for order compelling answers to form interrogatories and responses and production of documents; and for award of monetary sanctions granted as follows:
1. All objections are waived except attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
2. Plaintiff shall serve full, complete and verified responses to defendant's form interrogatories (set one) and demand for inspection (set one) within fifteen days this order, subject to stay as set forth herein;
3. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $290.00 to run against plaintiff only;
4. Imposition of this order is stayed to February 1, 2010.
Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Action
Off Calendar Nov-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LISA LANDEY Motion to Dismiss Action. Appearances: Lauren B. Gelber, Esq. Law Offices of Brian L. Larsen, (415) 398-5000 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Thomas J. Feeney, Esq. Law Offices of MacMorris & Carbone, (510) 627-7273 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. The defendant was added by amendment to complaint on May 8, 2009 and served less than one month later. The action is deemed commenced as to the new defendant from the date of the filing of the amendment. (See Hennessey's Tavern, Inc. vs. American Air Filter Co. (1988) 204 Cal.App. 3d 1351, 1357, where new defendant was allegedly alter ego of defendant named in original complaint.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
